title: Open GeoJSON in QGIS
excerpt: Work with lightweight web-friendly vector data and bring it into a desktop GIS workflow.
content:
## What GeoJSON is

GeoJSON is a text-based format for vector geographic data. It is widely used for sharing spatial layers between platforms and tools. A GeoJSON file contains both geometry and attribute data in a single file, which makes it simpler to handle than a shapefile.

## Steps

1. Open **Data Source Manager** from the Layer menu or press Ctrl+L.
2. Select **Vector** from the left panel.
3. Click the browse button next to the File Name field.
4. Navigate to your .geojson or .json file and select it.
5. Click **Add**.

The layer will appear in the Layers panel and display in the map view.

## After loading

- Zoom to the layer using right-click → **Zoom to Layer**.
- Open the attribute table to inspect the data fields.
- GeoJSON files are typically in WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) by default. Confirm this in layer properties if the data appears misaligned.

## Tip

GeoJSON can be opened directly from a URL in some versions of QGIS using the Vector data source type and entering the URL instead of a local file path. This is useful for layers served from a web endpoint.
